title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
Encode untrusted text and attribute values for their exact HTML context and reject unsafe script, style, comment, and URL placements.
Keep trusted renderer output distinct from untrusted author text and make every conversion into trusted markup explicit and reviewable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Render Determinism
steps.instruction:
Order the Render Determinism Static-site publishing and Python source-to-publication evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Render Determinism.

content.text: A debug publishing team handles case 6771 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.

Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the render determinism · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For render determinism · fault isolation, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Render Determinism
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-render-determinism-15cgfs" that uses case 4406 to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Render Determinism.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Render Determinism into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Render Determinism, keep Render Determinism connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-render-determinism-15cgfs
# Competency: ssg-render-determinism.
# Case 4406: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_render_determinism_15cgfs(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_15cgfs = "15cgfs-sfgc51-4406"
    assert evidence_variant_15cgfs
    evidence_axes_15cgfs = (
        "reader15cgfs",
        "source4406",
        "parsesfgc51",
        "route15cgfs4406",
        "artifact440615cgfs",
        "recoverysfgc514406",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_15cgfs)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"
